Home.title: Welcome to
Home.description: A community coordination platform for the Organic DAO. Propose ideas, vote on decisions, and collaborate on tasks.
Home.heroTitle: A governance and execution hub for
Home.heroSubtitle: Organic is a production DAO platform where token holders guide the treasury, shape proposals, and turn decisions into real work.
Home.holdTokensCallout: Hold $ORG tokens? Link your wallet and get your Organic ID!
Home.proposalsDescription: Submit ideas and vote on proposals with token-weighted voting.
Home.tasksDescription: Turn approved proposals into tasks and track progress with sprints.
Home.sprintsDescription: Plan milestones, assign owners, and ship work in focused cycles.
Home.governanceDescription: Vote with $ORG and create binding decisions for the DAO.
Home.organicIdDescription: Token holders get a unique ID and verified member status.
Home.heroContractNote: Use the official $ORG mint address to verify you are interacting with the authentic DAO token.
Home.heroAccessBody: Link a wallet holding $ORG to unlock governance, tasks, and contributor tools.
Home.poweredByOrgDescription: The Organic DAO is a community-driven organization on Solana. Hold $ORG tokens to participate in governance and shape the future.
Home.capabilitiesSubtitle: Everything you need to propose, decide, and deliver inside the DAO.
Home.activitySubtitle: Live updates from proposals, tasks, and member activity.
Home.statusDescription: Track your identity, governance access, and contribution footprint across the DAO.
Home.statusCardOneBody: Verify membership by linking a wallet that holds $ORG.
Home.statusCardTwoBody: Your votes and shipped tasks build a durable on-chain footprint.
Home.statusCardThreeBody: See how your proposals and tasks move the DAO forward.
Home.trustSubtitle: Live governance credibility signals across execution, voting, and contribution health.
Home.trustSprintNone: No sprint currently in an execution phase.
Home.trustSprintEndingDesc: The current sprint is ending -- act fast!
Home.carouselProposalsDesc: Submit bold ideas and shape the DAO's direction through token-weighted voting. Every proposal you create or vote on directly influences how the treasury is allocated and which initiatives move forward. Don't just watch -- lead.
Home.carouselProposalsFomo: Active proposals waiting for your vote right now
Home.carouselTasksDesc: Turn approved proposals into real, shipped work. Pick up tasks, earn XP and points, and climb the contributor leaderboard. The most active builders get recognized and rewarded every sprint.
Home.carouselTasksFomo: Open tasks ready to claim -- first come, first served
Home.carouselSprintsDesc: Work in focused cycles with clear deadlines and transparent settlement. Ship features, resolve disputes, and earn rewards. Each sprint is a chance to prove your impact and grow your reputation.
Home.carouselSprintsFomo: Current sprint in progress -- time is ticking
Home.carouselAnalyticsDesc: Track community health, governance throughput, and contribution trends across the entire DAO. See who's active, which proposals are gaining traction, and how the organization is growing in real time.
Home.carouselTreasuryDesc: Full transparency into community funds. Every SOL and $ORG movement is on-chain and auditable. See how the treasury is allocated, track settlements, and verify that governance decisions are being honored.
Home.carouselLeaderboardDesc: See who's leading the pack. XP-ranked contributors competing for recognition, rewards, and influence. Your position reflects your real impact -- every task, vote, and proposal counts.
Home.carouselLeaderboardFomo: Members competing for top spots right now
Home.navTasksDesc: Pick up work, earn XP and points

HowItWorks.title: How Organic works
HowItWorks.subtitle: Everything you need to know to start contributing
HowItWorks.xpBody: Earn XP by completing tasks, voting on proposals, sharing ideas, and posting. XP levels you up — higher levels unlock council access, AI tools, and NFT rewards.
HowItWorks.pointsBody: Points are your spending currency. Earn through engagement, spend to boost posts and promote ideas. Weekly cap keeps the economy healthy.
HowItWorks.pipelineBody: Ideas become Proposals. Approved Proposals create Tasks. Tasks get done in Sprints. Anyone can start by sharing an idea.
HowItWorks.xBody: Post about Organic on X to earn points. Your own posts are free (3/week). Engaging with others' Organic posts earns bonus XP.
HowItWorks.perksBody: Each level unlocks new abilities: create proposals at Lv3, AI tools at Lv7, council eligibility at Lv5, NFT minting at Lv11.

LaunchBanner.message: Launch Week — 2x XP on all actions!

Leaderboard.title: Leaderboard
Leaderboard.subtitle: Top contributors ranked by XP earned across verified contributions
Leaderboard.emptyDescription: Complete tasks to earn points and appear on the leaderboard!
Leaderboard.howRankingItem1: • XP is the primary ranking signal on the leaderboard
Leaderboard.howRankingItem2: • If XP ties, total points break the tie
Leaderboard.howRankingItem3: • If XP and points tie, completed tasks break the tie
Leaderboard.howRankingItem4: • Points still matter for rewards and profile context
Leaderboard.howPointsItem1: • Complete assigned tasks to earn points
Leaderboard.howPointsItem2: • Each task has a point value based on its complexity
Leaderboard.howPointsItem3: • Points are automatically added when tasks are marked as done
Leaderboard.howPointsItem4: • Higher points = Higher ranking on the leaderboard
Leaderboard.infoSection1Point1: **XP is the primary ranking signal.** Every task you complete, every vote you cast, every governance action you take earns experience points that push you up the board.
Leaderboard.infoSection1Point2: **Ties break by total points, then by tasks completed.** So if two members have identical XP, the one who's shipped more work ranks higher.
Leaderboard.infoSection1Point3: **Rankings update after each verified contribution.** There's no daily reset — your position reflects your cumulative impact in real time.
Leaderboard.infoSection2Point1: **Levels represent commitment milestones.** Each level requires more XP than the last, so reaching Level 5 means significantly more than reaching Level 2.
Leaderboard.infoSection2Point2: **Your level is visible across the platform** — on your profile card, in task comments, and next to your votes. It signals trustworthiness to the community.
Leaderboard.infoSection2Point3: **Higher levels unlock new badges and recognition.** These aren't cosmetic — they reflect real work verified on-chain and by peer review.
Leaderboard.infoSection3Point1: **Your leaderboard position is your on-chain contribution footprint.** It's visible proof of the work you've done, not just the tokens you hold.
Leaderboard.infoSection3Point2: **The podium highlights the top 3 contributors** each sprint. Consistent top performers build the kind of reputation that carries weight in governance votes and task assignments.

Proposals.title: Proposals
Proposals.subtitle: Submit ideas and vote on proposals for the Organic DAO
Proposals.discoveryTitle: Discover proposals by stage and intent
Proposals.discoverySubtitle: Use lifecycle and category filters to find where your participation has the highest impact.
Proposals.listSubtitle: {count, plural, one {# proposal visible} other {# proposals visible}}
Proposals.cardCommentsSignal: {count, plural, one {# comment in thread} other {# comments in thread}}
Proposals.ctaDescription: Sign in and link your wallet to create proposals and vote on decisions.
Proposals.emptySearchDescription: No proposals match "{search}". Try a different search.
Proposals.emptyTitle: Be the first to start a discussion
Proposals.infoSection1Point1: **Proposals are the heartbeat of governance.** Any member with an Organic ID can submit a proposal to suggest features, allocate treasury funds, or shape the DAO's direction.
Proposals.infoSection1Point2: Each proposal moves through stages: **Public → Qualified → Discussion → Voting → Finalized.** Community engagement at every stage determines what moves forward.
Proposals.infoSection1Point3: Proposals that reach the voting stage are decided by the full community. One member, one vote — transparent and on-the-record.
Proposals.infoSection2Point1: **Vote on active proposals** to shape the DAO's future. Every vote earns XP and moves you up the governance track.
Proposals.infoSection2Point2: **Comment on discussions** to refine proposals before they go to vote. Thoughtful feedback is rewarded with XP.
Proposals.infoSection2Point3: **Submit your own proposals** when you see an opportunity to improve the DAO. Great proposals start with a clear problem and a concrete solution.
Proposals.infoSection3Point1: Use the **category filters** to focus on what matters to you — Treasury, Governance, Community, Development, or Feature proposals.
Proposals.infoSection3Point2: The **Governance Sidebar** shows the pulse of current activity — hot topics, stage distribution, and community engagement trends.

taskTypeDescriptions.twitter: Verify likes, retweets, and comments on X

Tasks.twitterTargetRequired: Twitter/X tasks require a target tweet URL
Tasks.twitterInstructionsPlaceholder: Add specific guidance for this Twitter/X task...
Tasks.infoSection1Point1: **Tasks are the building blocks of this DAO.** Every piece of work — from writing code to creating content to moderating discussions — lives here as a task with clear scope, deadline, and reward.
Tasks.infoSection1Point2: **Admins and council members create tasks.** Each task has a type (development, design, content, Twitter/X, or custom), a priority level, and a point value that reflects its weight.
Tasks.infoSection1Point3: **Tasks can link to governance proposals.** When a proposal passes, the work it requires shows up here — creating a direct line from community decision to contributor action.
Tasks.infoSection2Point1: **1. Discover** — Browse, search, and filter tasks by status, priority, type, or sprint. Find work that matches your skills.
Tasks.infoSection2Point2: **2. Claim → Submit** — Claim a task to lock it in. Complete the work and submit your deliverable (code link, file upload, or proof of engagement depending on task type).
Tasks.infoSection2Point3: **3. Review → Complete** — A reviewer checks your submission. Approved? You earn the points and XP. Rejected? You get a reason and can resubmit.
Tasks.infoSection3Point1: **Every completed task earns you XP and points.** XP builds your level and leaderboard rank. Points convert to $ORG token rewards at the end of each sprint.
Tasks.infoSection3Point2: **Quality matters.** Reviewers rate submissions 1–5 stars. Higher quality scores boost your reputation and unlock future opportunities.
Tasks.infoSection3Point3: **Dependencies and subtasks keep work organized.** Large tasks can be broken into subtasks, and tasks can depend on each other — so the right work happens in the right order.
Tasks.earnCtaDescription: Claim tasks, submit work, and earn $ORG tokens. Every contribution counts.
Tasks.earnCtaInline: Want to earn $ORG tokens? <link>Create an account</link> to claim tasks and start contributing.

ReviewQueue.title: Submission review queue
ReviewQueue.subtitle: Review pending task submissions

TaskDetail.alertAdminOnlyDelete: Only admin members can delete tasks
TaskDetail.errorLoadTaskDescription: We couldn't load this task. Please try again or go back to the task list.
TaskDetail.noComments: No comments yet. Be the first to comment!
TaskDetail.assignDescription: Select a user to assign this task to:
TaskDetail.deleteDescription: Are you sure you want to delete this task? This action cannot be undone and will also delete all comments associated with this task.
TaskDetail.dependenciesOpenCount: {count, plural, one {# open blocker} other {# open blockers}}
TaskDetail.dependencyHint: This task tracks {count, plural, one {# linked dependency} other {# linked dependencies}}. Clear blockers before final review.
TaskDetail.infoSection1Point1: **Status tells you where this task is in its lifecycle** — open (available), in progress (someone's working on it), in review (submitted, waiting for approval), or completed.
TaskDetail.infoSection1Point2: **Points and XP show what you'll earn** when your submission is approved. Higher-value tasks require more effort but boost your rank faster.
TaskDetail.infoSection1Point3: **Acceptance criteria define what 'done' looks like.** Read these before you start — reviewers check your work against them.
TaskDetail.infoSection2Point1: **Claim the task to signal you're working on it.** This prevents duplicate effort. You can unclaim if plans change — no penalty.
TaskDetail.infoSection2Point2: **Submit your work using the form below.** Each task type has its own submission format — code tasks want a link, design tasks want a file, Twitter tasks verify your engagement.
TaskDetail.infoSection2Point3: **You can resubmit if rejected.** The reviewer will tell you why it was turned down. Fix the issue and try again.
TaskDetail.infoSection3Point1: **A reviewer or admin evaluates your work** against the acceptance criteria. They'll approve, reject with feedback, or request changes.
TaskDetail.infoSection3Point2: **Approved submissions earn you the full point and XP value** listed on this task. Quality ratings (1–5 stars) from the reviewer also factor into your reputation.
TaskDetail.infoSection3Point3: **Completed tasks appear on your profile** as part of your contribution history — building your on-chain reputation over time.
TaskDetail.earningsDisclaimer: XP estimates based on task value. Actual XP may vary based on review quality rating.

Analytics.heroMetricsDesc: Track member growth and activity trends
Analytics.heroTasksDesc: Monitor task completion and contributor output
Analytics.kpiPriceHelper: Price data available after token listing
Analytics.kpiMarketCapHelper: Market cap available after token listing
Analytics.personalPlaceholder: Your personal analytics dashboard is coming soon. Track your submissions, points, activity, and followed tasks all in one place.
Analytics.personalLockedTitle: Sign in to view personal analytics
Analytics.personalLockedDesc: Sign in to access your personal analytics dashboard with submission history, points tracking, and activity insights.
Analytics.infoSection1Point1: **The top cards are your DAO's vital signs.** Total users, token holders, completed tasks, and active proposals — updated live from your database and the Solana blockchain.
Analytics.infoSection1Point2: **Sparkline bars show directional trends**, not exact history. Green means up, gray means flat. Use them to spot momentum shifts at a glance.
Analytics.infoSection1Point3: **Numbers refresh on every page load.** There's no caching delay — what you see is what's happening right now.
Analytics.infoSection2Point1: **The activity chart tracks 30 days of contributions** — tasks completed, governance actions, and comments. Spikes usually align with sprint deadlines or active proposals.
Analytics.infoSection2Point2: **Member growth shows your community's trajectory** over 12 months. Sustained growth here means your onboarding and retention are working.
Analytics.infoSection2Point3: **Task completions measure weekly output** from your contributor base. This is the clearest signal of whether work is actually getting done.
Analytics.infoSection3Point1: **Proposal categories reveal where your DAO focuses attention.** A healthy distribution across governance, treasury, and development means balanced decision-making.
Analytics.infoSection3Point2: **Voting participation is the quality signal.** High proposal counts with low voter turnout means decisions are being made by a small group — worth watching.
Analytics.infoSection3Point3: **The trust strip at the top surfaces 30-day throughput**, dispute rates, and active contributor counts — your governance health at a glance.

personal.xpTrendDesc: Daily XP earned over the last 90 days
personal.heatmapDesc: Your daily activity over the past year

aiSummary.title: AI Governance Digest
aiSummary.noSummary: No governance summary available yet. Summaries are generated daily.

Treasury.heroDescription: The Organic DAO treasury funds community initiatives, development, and ecosystem growth. All spending requires governance approval through the proposal system.
Treasury.latestSettlementDescription: Most recent reward settlement posture and integrity status.
Treasury.transparencyDescription: Emission constraints, settlement outcomes, and audit visibility are surfaced here to reduce trust ambiguity.
Treasury.infoSection1Point1: **This wallet holds the DAO's SOL and $ORG tokens.** All balances are fetched live from the Solana blockchain — not from a database, from the actual chain.
Treasury.infoSection1Point2: **Hold the lock button for 1.5 seconds to reveal the full wallet address.** This interaction exists because the address is sensitive — it shouldn't be copy-pasteable by accident.
Treasury.infoSection1Point3: **Everything here is verifiable.** You can take the wallet address and check it on any Solana explorer. The numbers you see here will match.
Treasury.infoSection2Point1: **The emission policy controls how many tokens are distributed each sprint.** There's a fixed cap per cycle, set by governance, to prevent inflation.
Treasury.infoSection2Point2: **Settlement is when rewards actually get paid out.** After a sprint ends and submissions are reviewed, the settlement engine distributes tokens to contributors based on their approved work.
Treasury.infoSection2Point3: **All treasury spending requires a governance proposal.** No admin can unilaterally move funds — every transaction is traceable and community-approved.
Treasury.infoSection3Point1: **Fund allocation targets are set by community vote.** The DAO decides how much goes to development, operations, and contributor rewards each epoch.
Treasury.infoSection3Point2: **Audit trails are available for every settlement.** When tokens move, there's a record of why, who approved it, and which sprint it belongs to.
Treasury.disclaimer: Balances and transactions are fetched live from the Solana blockchain. Allocation percentages are targets and may differ from actual holdings.
Treasury.loadError: Unable to load treasury data. Please try again later.

general.name: Organization Name

general.orgDescriptionHelp: A short description of your organization

token.analyticsDescription: Configure how token analytics are displayed — LP vault exclusions and chart embed settings.
token.lpVaultsDescription: Wallet addresses of DEX liquidity pool vaults to exclude from holder distribution analysis. These hold tokens as liquidity, not as individual holders.
token.dexPairDescription: The DexScreener pair address for chart embeds. Leave empty to auto-resolve from token mint.

treasury.walletDescription: Solana wallet address for the DAO treasury
treasury.allocationsDescription: How treasury funds are allocated. Must total 100%.

governance.quorumDescription: Minimum participation required for a valid vote
governance.approvalDescription: Percentage of YES votes needed to pass
governance.proposalThresholdDescription: Minimum tokens required to create a proposal
governance.cooldownDescription: Days a proposer must wait between proposals
governance.abstainQuorumDescription: Whether abstain votes count toward meeting quorum
governance.qualificationThresholdDescription: Percent of active Organic IDs required to auto-qualify a proposal
governance.antiSpamHoursDescription: Minimum hours between proposal publications by the same proposer
governance.overrideTtlDescription: Days before an admin/council promotion override auto-reverts
governance.overrideCouncilReviewDescription: Require council review flag for override promotions
governance.overrideCouncilReviewLabel: Require council review for override promotions
governance.warningQuorum: Changing quorum affects how many participants are needed for a valid vote. Low values may allow proposals to pass with minimal participation.
governance.warningApproval: Changing the approval threshold affects how easily proposals pass. Low values may allow controversial proposals to succeed.
governance.warningDuration: Changing voting duration affects all future proposals. Short windows may not give members enough time to participate.

sprints.description: Sprint defaults and Organic ID configuration
sprints.capacityDescription: Default point capacity for new sprints
sprints.organicIdThresholdDescription: Minimum tokens required for Organic ID eligibility (0 = no minimum)
sprints.disputeWindowHoursDescription: Default dispute submission window after review starts
sprints.reviewerSlaHoursDescription: Hours a reviewer has before escalation is triggered
sprints.reviewerSlaExtensionHoursDescription: Additional hours granted after automatic escalation

rewards.description: Configure the rewards and token distribution system
rewards.enabledDescription: Allow members to claim token rewards for points earned
rewards.rateDescription: How many points equal 1 ORG token
rewards.minThresholdDescription: Minimum points required to submit a claim
rewards.defaultEpochPoolDescription: Default token pool for sprint reward distributions
rewards.requireWalletDescription: Users must have a linked wallet to claim rewards
rewards.settlementEmissionPercentDescription: Fraction of treasury balance eligible for sprint settlement emissions (0-1)
rewards.settlementFixedCapDescription: Hard ORG cap per sprint settlement
rewards.settlementCarryoverCapDescription: Maximum consecutive sprints carryover can roll forward
rewards.treasuryBalanceForEmissionDescription: Reference treasury balance used for emission-cap calculations

admin.payoutGuardrailsBody: Confirm claim status, payout amount, and tx signature before marking a claim as paid.
admin.distributionIntegrityBody: Keep manual corrections rare and documented. Settlement holds or kill-switch states should be resolved before new payout waves.
admin.manualDescription: Send token rewards directly to contributors for bonuses, bounties, or corrections.

Disputes.title: Disputes
Disputes.pageDescription: Review and resolve task submission disputes.
Disputes.createDescription: Dispute a task submission review. An XP stake is required.
Disputes.queueEmptyHint: Disputes filed by members will appear here for arbitration.
Disputes.myDisputesEmptyHint: If you disagree with a task review, you can file a dispute from the task detail page.
Disputes.withdrawConfirm: Are you sure? A withdrawal fee of {fee} XP will be deducted from your stake.
Disputes.appealConfirm: Appeal this ruling? An admin will make the final decision.
Disputes.recuseConfirm: Remove yourself as arbitrator? The dispute will return to the queue.
Disputes.xpStakeInfo: This XP will be deducted now. It will be refunded if the dispute is upheld or mediated.
Disputes.xpInsufficient: Insufficient XP to file a dispute
Disputes.cooldownActive: Cooldown active — wait {days} days between disputes
Disputes.minXpRequired: Minimum {xp} XP required to file disputes

impact.submissionApprovalLine: Submission approved at {score}/5 (+{points} points, about +{xp} XP).
impact.disputantStakeLockedLine: Disputant: stake remains spent (no XP refund).
impact.reviewerNoPenaltyLine: Reviewer: no XP penalty on this outcome.
impact.arbitratorNoRewardLine: Arbitrator reward is skipped until one is assigned.
impact.mediationPendingLine: Mediation proposal submitted. Waiting for the other party confirmation.
impact.questHintLine: Progression quests refresh automatically after this action.
impact.noNetXpLine: No additional XP changes are applied right now.

form.evidencePlaceholder: Describe why you believe the review was unfair. Provide specific details and context...
form.requestMediationHint: Try to resolve with the reviewer before escalating (24h window)
form.responsePlaceholder: Provide your perspective on why the original review was fair...
form.resolutionNotesPlaceholder: Explain the reasoning behind your decision...
form.newQualityScoreHint: Set a new quality score for the compromise
form.appealReasonPlaceholder: Explain why you believe the council ruling was incorrect...
form.responseGuardrail: Keep this response factual, cite evidence links, and address each review concern directly.
form.resolutionGuardrail: Resolution notes must explain evidence weighting, final quality posture, and policy rationale.

detail.limitedAccess: You are viewing limited information. Full details are available to dispute parties.
detail.responseNoDeadline: No reviewer response deadline has been set.

integrity.escalationRecommended: Escalation is recommended because reviewer response SLA is overdue.
integrity.responseStatusHint: Monitor for response submission or tier reassignment before final resolution.
integrity.mediationActive: This dispute is currently in mediation tier.
integrity.councilPath: This dispute is currently routed to council arbitration.
integrity.adminPath: This dispute is currently routed to admin final review.
integrity.mediationHint: Path visibility keeps parties aligned on who resolves next and why.

triage.commandDeckSubtitle: Use SLA posture and tier routing to clear blockers quickly.

triage.createGuardrail: Before filing, ensure your evidence is specific, verifiable, and tied to task requirements.

Ideas.title: Community Ideas
Ideas.subtitle: Start lightweight discussions, vote early, and surface the best ideas before they become formal proposals.
Ideas.spotlightEmpty: No spotlight idea yet this week.
Ideas.empty: No ideas match your filters yet.
Ideas.emptyDescription: Be the one to start the conversation. Share an idea the community can rally behind.
Ideas.feedError: Failed to load ideas. Please try again.
Ideas.composerSubtitle: Pitch a concept the community can discuss and vote on.
Ideas.composerBodyPlaceholder: Describe your idea and why it matters...
Ideas.organicRequired: You need an Organic ID to post and interact with ideas.
Ideas.signinPrompt: Sign in to vote and join the discussion.
Ideas.disabledDescription: Enable the feature flag to access the ideas experience.
Ideas.heroPromoteDesc: Best ideas become formal governance proposals

IdeaDetail.disabledDescription: Enable the feature flag to access this page.
IdeaDetail.notFoundDescription: This idea may have been removed or does not exist.
IdeaDetail.errorDescription: We could not load this idea. Please try again.
IdeaDetail.linkedProposalLabel: This idea is now linked to a proposal
IdeaDetail.promoteDescription: Promoting this idea will create a proposal draft linked to it. The community can then vote on the formal proposal.
IdeaDetail.signInPrompt: Sign in to join the discussion.
IdeaDetail.commentsEmpty: No comments yet. Start the thread.
IdeaDetail.timelineVotes: {count, plural, one {# vote received} other {# votes received}}
IdeaDetail.infoSection1Point1: **Ideas are lightweight proposals** — a space to float concepts before committing to a formal governance proposal. Think of it as the DAO's brainstorming board.
IdeaDetail.infoSection1Point2: Upvote ideas you believe in. The most supported ideas get **promoted to full proposals** and enter the governance pipeline.
IdeaDetail.infoSection1Point3: Ideas with momentum earn their authors XP and achievement progress. Top ideas are featured in the **Weekly Harvest** spotlight.
IdeaDetail.infoSection2Point1: **Submit ideas** about anything — features, partnerships, events, governance improvements. No idea is too small to start a conversation.
IdeaDetail.infoSection2Point2: **Vote and comment** to help the best ideas rise. Your engagement here directly shapes what the DAO works on next.
IdeaDetail.infoSection2Point3: Check the **Trending** tab to see what's generating buzz, or browse **Promoted** to see which ideas made it to proposals.
IdeaDetail.infoSection3Point1: Creating ideas, voting, and commenting all earn **XP and quest progress.** Daily quests like "Share an Idea" and "Vote on Ideas" reward consistent participation.
IdeaDetail.infoSection3Point2: The **Idea Champion** quest rewards having an idea promoted to a proposal — the ultimate validation from your community.

Harvest.title: Weekly Harvest
Harvest.subtitle: A curated look at this week's best ideas, top contributors, and community momentum.
Harvest.disabledDescription: Enable the feature flag to access this page.
Harvest.noWinner: No ideas submitted this week yet. Be the first!
Harvest.noContributors: No XP earned this week yet.
Harvest.nextWeekDescription: Share an idea and get 3 upvotes to earn the Community Voice quest bonus. Top contributor earns a spotlight badge!
Harvest.infoSection1Point1: **Every week, the best ideas are spotlighted here.** The winning idea, top contributors, and community stats are curated into a single page.
Harvest.infoSection1Point2: This is where momentum becomes visible — see who's driving the DAO forward and what concepts are gaining traction.
Harvest.infoSection2Point1: **Submit ideas that resonate.** Ideas with the most upvotes and engagement rise to the top of the weekly spotlight.
Harvest.infoSection2Point2: **Stay active.** Consistent contributors who vote, comment, and propose ideas earn spotlight recognition and bonus XP.

Community.title: Community
Community.subtitle: Meet the people building the Organic DAO — explore rankings and the member directory.
Community.leaderboardError: Unable to load rankings. The leaderboard data is temporarily unavailable.
Community.searchRankings: Search rankings by name or ID...
Community.infoSection1Point1: **Rankings reflect real contribution, not just activity.** XP earned from tasks, governance, and community engagement determines your position on the leaderboard.
Community.infoSection1Point2: Levels unlock new abilities — higher levels can create proposals, moderate content, and access exclusive quests.
Community.infoSection1Point3: Your rank updates in real time as you and others earn XP. Every action counts.
Community.infoSection2Point1: **Browse the full member list** to find collaborators, see who's active, and explore individual profiles with contribution history.
Community.infoSection2Point2: Each member card shows their role, level, XP, and recent activity — a quick snapshot of their DAO involvement.
Community.infoSection3Point1: **Complete tasks** for the biggest XP gains. Development, content, and design tasks all contribute to your rank.
Community.infoSection3Point2: **Participate in governance** — voting, commenting, and proposing ideas all earn XP and boost your standing.

Posts.title: Community Posts
Posts.subtitle: Share updates, start discussions, and connect with the community.
Posts.emptyState: No posts yet. Be the first to share!
Posts.likeError: You must be logged in to like posts
Posts.infoSection1Point1: **Posts are the community's open forum.** Share updates, start discussions, drop links, or publish threaded deep-dives — this is where the DAO talks.
Posts.infoSection1Point2: Unlike Ideas (which feed governance), posts are for **general conversation** — project updates, ecosystem news, questions, and celebrations.
Posts.infoSection1Point3: Posts, threads, announcements, and link shares each serve a different purpose. Use the type filters to find what you need.
Posts.infoSection2Point1: **Creating posts costs points** (5-12 pts for non-organic). Organic-related posts get 3 free per week. Points are claimable for ORG tokens.
Posts.infoSection2Point2: **Organic posts earn bonus rewards.** Authors earn points when others like (+1 pt) or comment (+2 pts) on their organic posts. Everyone earns XP from engagement.
Posts.heroDescription: Share updates, start discussions, and connect with the community.
Posts.composerLinkHint: Every post needs a link. Share a tweet, article, or any URL.
Posts.composerOrganicHint: Posts about Organic DAO get bonus rewards
Posts.composerInsufficientPoints: Not enough points to create this post
Posts.flagConfirm: Flag this post as not organic-related?
Posts.promoteSpotlightDesc: 24h gold highlight, pinned in Popular, 1.5x rewards
Posts.organicBonusRevoked: Organic bonus removed by community flags

NotFound.heading: Lost in the garden?
NotFound.description: This page has been moved or doesn't exist anymore. Let us help you find your way.

AdminDashboard.title: Admin Operations
AdminDashboard.subtitle: Operational overview and quick actions
AdminDashboard.accessDeniedDesc: You need admin or council permissions to access the admin dashboard.
AdminDashboard.accessDeniedExplanation: This area is for admins and council members. If you believe you should have access, contact your organization admin.

timeline.emptyDesc: Configuration changes will appear here as they happen.

Dashboard.step1Desc: The dashboard is your home base. It shows the hero section with a quick overview of what Organic is — a visibility-first coordination platform where real community work is made public and verifiable.
Dashboard.step2Desc: Four live cards show key DAO health signals at a glance: the current sprint countdown, proposal stages, top contributors on the leaderboard, and recent activity count. This is your real-time trust surface.
Dashboard.step3Desc: Below the trust pulse, navigation cards let you jump directly into Tasks, Proposals, Sprints, Analytics, and more. Each card shows a brief description so you know what to expect.
Dashboard.step4Desc: This section shows your identity within the DAO — whether you're signed in, if you have an Organic ID, and your governance access level. Link a wallet holding $ORG to unlock full contributor capabilities.
Dashboard.step5Desc: A compact stats bar at the bottom summarizes platform-wide metrics: total tasks, active sprints, members, and proposals. Hover over any bar for more detail.

Tasks.step1Desc: The tasks page is where all DAO work lives. Browse open tasks, filter by category or sprint, and find work that matches your skills.
Tasks.step2Desc: Tasks flow through stages: Open → Claimed → In Review → Approved. Each status is color-coded so you can see progress at a glance.
Tasks.step3Desc: Found something you want to work on? Click the task card to see details, then hit Claim to assign it to yourself. You need an Organic ID to claim tasks.
Tasks.step4Desc: When you're done, submit your work for review. Attach proof (links, screenshots, descriptions) so reviewers can verify your contribution.
Tasks.step5Desc: Each task has a point value. Approved tasks earn you XP that builds your reputation and unlocks higher-tier work.
Tasks.step6Desc: Use the filter bar to narrow tasks by category, contributor, sprint, or search term. Sort by newest, due date, or point value to find what matters most.

Proposals.step1Desc: Proposals are how the DAO makes decisions. Anyone with an Organic ID can submit a proposal for the community to consider.
Proposals.step2Desc: Proposals flow through stages: Public → Qualified → Discussion → Voting → Finalized. Each stage has specific requirements before advancing.
Proposals.step3Desc: Click 'New Proposal' to start. Provide a title, description, category, and what you're proposing. The community will review and discuss before voting.
Proposals.step4Desc: New proposals need community engagement to qualify for voting. Sufficient comments, likes, and views signal the community wants to vote on it.
Proposals.step5Desc: Qualified proposals enter a voting window. Token holders cast votes weighted by their $ORG holdings. Results are binding — approved proposals become actionable.
Proposals.step6Desc: Sort proposals by New, Hot, Most Discussed, or Most Voted to find what's active. Filter by category to focus on your area of interest.

Sprints.step1Desc: Sprints are focused execution cycles where the DAO ships work. They group tasks into time-boxed periods with clear start and end dates.
Sprints.step2Desc: Sprints progress through phases: Planning → Active → Review → Dispute Window → Settlement. Each phase has specific rules for what can happen.
Sprints.step3Desc: Switch between Board, List, and Timeline views to see sprint work in the format that suits you best. Board shows Kanban-style columns, List is compact, Timeline shows dates.
Sprints.step4Desc: Admins advance sprints through phases. When a sprint moves to Review, all submitted work gets evaluated. The dispute window allows challenges before final settlement.
Sprints.step5Desc: Each sprint has a capacity limit. The progress bar shows how much of the sprint's point budget is allocated, helping the team plan realistic workloads.

Rewards.step1Desc: The rewards page tracks how contributions turn into payouts. View your earned points, pending claims, and payout history.
Rewards.step2Desc: Approved tasks earn you points. Points accumulate and become claimable when they reach the threshold set by the DAO.
Rewards.step3Desc: When you have enough points, submit a claim. Your claim goes through an approval process before the payout is released.
Rewards.step4Desc: Claims are reviewed by council members. Approved claims enter the payout queue. The treasury processes payouts in batches.
Rewards.step5Desc: The Distributions tab shows all completed payouts. Track when payments were made, amounts, and transaction status.

Disputes.step1Desc: Disputes exist to keep the DAO fair. If you believe a task review was incorrect or a decision was unfair, you can file a dispute.
Disputes.step2Desc: File a dispute during the sprint's dispute window. You need to stake XP to file — this prevents frivolous disputes and shows you're serious.
Disputes.step3Desc: Filing a dispute requires staking XP. If the dispute is upheld, you get your stake back plus the resolution. If denied, you lose the stake.
Disputes.step4Desc: Council members review disputes and vote on the outcome. The process is transparent — all parties can see the reasoning behind the decision.
Disputes.step5Desc: Once arbitration is complete, the decision is binding. Task reviews may be overturned, points adjusted, and XP stakes settled accordingly.

Quests.step1Desc: Quests are gamified challenges that reward consistent participation. Complete daily, weekly, and long-term objectives to earn bonus XP.
Quests.step2Desc: Small, daily actions like logging in, commenting on a proposal, or reviewing a task submission. These reset every 24 hours.
Quests.step3Desc: Larger objectives that span a week: complete a task, vote on proposals, or participate in discussions. Higher XP rewards for weekly consistency.
Quests.step4Desc: Achievement-style quests for sustained contribution: complete your first sprint, earn a reputation tier, or hit a streak milestone.
Quests.step5Desc: Quest XP adds to your total reputation score. The cadence system rewards consistency — maintaining streaks multiplies your quest rewards over time.

Analytics.step1Desc: Analytics provides a data-driven view of DAO health. Track activity trends, member growth, task velocity, and governance participation.
Analytics.step2Desc: The activity chart shows daily/weekly event volume — task completions, proposal submissions, votes, and comments. Spot patterns and engagement cycles.
Analytics.step3Desc: Switch to the Personal tab to see your own contribution metrics: tasks completed, proposals voted on, XP earned, and your activity streak.
Analytics.step4Desc: The Governance tab tracks proposal outcomes, voter participation rates, and decision quality. A healthy DAO shows consistent engagement across all stages.
Analytics.step5Desc: Key performance indicators at the top give you instant snapshots: total members, active contributors, tasks this sprint, and governance participation rate.

Treasury.step1Desc: The treasury page shows how the DAO manages its funds. Transparency is core to Organic — anyone can inspect balances and allocation principles.
Treasury.step2Desc: See the current treasury balance and how funds are allocated across categories: operations, rewards, development, and reserves.
Treasury.step3Desc: The emission policy defines how tokens flow from treasury to contributors. This controls the rate of reward distribution to maintain sustainability.
Treasury.step4Desc: A ledger of all treasury movements — inflows, outflows, reward payouts, and operational expenses. Each entry is timestamped and categorized.
Treasury.step5Desc: Treasury operations are verifiable on-chain. The press-and-hold reveal feature lets you inspect sensitive details only when you intentionally choose to look.

Community.step1Desc: The community page showcases the people behind the DAO. See who's contributing, how they rank, and explore the member directory.
Community.step2Desc: Rankings are based on verified XP earned through real contributions — not just token holdings. The leaderboard updates in real-time as work gets approved.
Community.step3Desc: Browse all members, their roles, contribution history, and reputation scores. Find collaborators or see who's active in specific areas.
Community.step4Desc: Members are ranked by consistency and quality of contribution. The system rewards sustained participation over bursts of activity.

Ideas.step1Desc: Ideas are lightweight pre-proposal discussions. Share rough concepts, get community feedback, and refine before committing to a formal proposal.
Ideas.step2Desc: Post an idea with a title and description. Keep it casual — ideas are meant to be exploratory. The community will vote and comment to shape direction.
Ideas.step3Desc: Upvote ideas you think deserve attention. The vote count helps surface the most promising concepts and signals community interest to the author.
Ideas.step4Desc: Sort by Hot, New, Top This Week, or Top All Time. The Trending tab highlights ideas gaining rapid momentum.
Ideas.step5Desc: When an idea has enough traction, the author can promote it to a formal proposal. This carries over the discussion context and community support signals.

Posts.step1Desc: Points are your spendable currency — earn them from tasks, quests, and organic posts. Claim them for ORG tokens when ready. XP tracks your growth — everything earns XP, and leveling up unlocks airdrops, NFTs, and access to higher-value tasks.
Posts.step2Desc: Complete bounty tasks, post organic content, and let the community engage with your posts. Daily and weekly quests also reward points. Organic posts earn bonus points when others like and comment.
Posts.step3Desc: Creating non-organic posts costs points (keeping the feed spam-free). You can also promote your posts for extra visibility — promoted posts get highlighted and earn bonus rewards from engagement.
Posts.step4Desc: Posts about Organic DAO get 3 free per week and earn bonus rewards. Toggle "Organic-related" in the composer. The community can flag posts that aren't truly organic — 3 flags removes the bonus.
Posts.step5Desc: New to the community? Start with free organic posts, engage with others, and check the quests page. Your first task bounty will give you enough points to start creating paid content and promoting posts.

Marketplace.step1Desc: The Engagement Marketplace lets you spend points to boost your tweets. Other members earn points by engaging with your content — likes, retweets, and comments. Points are held in escrow and paid out when engagement is verified.
Marketplace.step2Desc: Browse active boosts and engage with tweets to earn points. Submit proof of your engagement (like, retweet, or comment) and receive points once verified. The more you engage, the more you earn.

Marketplace.title: Engagement Marketplace
Marketplace.description: Boost your tweets by spending points. Other members earn points by engaging with your content — likes, retweets, and comments.
Marketplace.pointsHint: Points are held in escrow and paid out to engagers. Min 5, max 1000.
Marketplace.proofUrlHint: Optional: link to your retweet or comment for verification.
Marketplace.noActiveBoosts: No active boosts right now. Be the first to create one!
Marketplace.noMyBoosts: You haven't created any boosts yet.
Marketplace.disabledDesc: The engagement marketplace is not yet available. Check back later!

ForProjects.heroSubtitle: The same platform powering Organic DAO — governance, tasks, rewards, and analytics — ready for your project. Community OS, not just another dashboard.
ForProjects.socialProof: Built by the Organic community. Battle-tested with real governance and real contributors.
ForProjects.tiersSubtitle: Limited to 10 beta projects — early adopters get priority support
ForProjects.tier_grove_desc: For growing projects with active governance
ForProjects.tier_forest_desc: For established DAOs needing the full stack
ForProjects.ctaSubtitle: Limited beta spots available. Get your platform set up with white-glove onboarding.
ForProjects.ctaFootnote: We'll respond within 48 hours. No commitment required.

Error.title: Something went wrong
Error.description: An unexpected error occurred. You can try again or go back to the home page.
Error.proposalDescription: There was an error loading the proposal. It may have been removed or you may not have access.
